description:
## Question

How is the `voice` feature declared in the feature seam, and how does every Mac end up carrying it?

Decide:
- Structural feature (nebula.features list in modules/hosts.nix, read outside home scope like mac/llm/tailscale) vs content-feature (modules/content-features.nix registry). It gates a darwin homebrew cask (outside home scope) → strongly indicates structural per CLAUDE.md.
- 'Active on all Macs': add an explicit `voice` to each Mac host's features list, or derive it from machine.features.mac so no host edits are needed? Weigh explicitness vs DRY against how other cross-Mac features are done.
- Where the feature's enable is wired (darwin-modules/features/default.nix, mirroring music/llm/tailscale).

HITL grilling / domain-modeling.

comments.text:
RESOLVED (grilling). Feature-seam model for the 'voice' feature:

1. STRUCTURAL feature (not content): it gates a Homebrew cask (outside home scope); modules/content-features.nix explicitly excludes this class. Register 'voice' in nebula.features (modules/hosts.nix) alongside llm/tailscale.

2. PER-HOST, EXPLICIT opt-in: add 'voice' to each Mac host's features list (spectre, ash) — greppable, per-host opt-out, mirrors llm/tailscale. NOT derived from machine.features.mac, so a Mac can decline it.

3. ENABLE WIRING: new module darwin-modules/features/voice.nix (mirroring music.nix/llm.nix), imported + wired in darwin-modules/features/default.nix as 'voice.enable = machine.features.voice;'.

4. MODULE OWNERSHIP: voice.nix owns the ENTIRE integration — the superwhisper cask AND the Claude Code hook + statusline settings — all under mkIf cfg.enable (gated on voice.enable alone).

5. INDEPENDENCE CONTRACT (honors map's locked Independent coupling): voice.nix contributes claude-code settings unconditionally w.r.t. claude-code and NEVER sets claude-code.enable. The contribution is inert wherever claude-code is disabled, because the settings.json write is gated by programs.claude-code.enable (verified: home-modules/settings/programs/claude-code.nix:179 gates the settings value; the upstream program module writes settings.json only when enabled; enable is set by firefly policy / claude-glm / ai). Net: cask -> all Macs; hooks -> materialize only where claude-code already lives (firefly Macs today: spectre, ash).

Note for nix-4t3.3: voice.nix must merge into programs.claude-code.settings.hooks (existing block at claude-code.nix:226) via the darwin 'user.*' alias, and add the SuperWhisper statusline. No cross-scope gating needed — voice.enable is the only condition.

title: Model the voice feature: structural vs content, and how Macs get it
